studyName: Host Genetic Determinants of the Outcome of SAB by WES

tables.description: The subject consent file includes subject IDs, consent information, subject aliases, and case control status of the subject for Staphylococcus Aureus Bacteremia (SAB).

variables.name: AFFECTION_STATUS
variables.description: Case control status of the subject for Staphylococcus Aureus Bacteremia (SAB); Case =Complicated SAB, Control = Uncomplicated SAB

variables.name: CONSENT
variables.description: Consent group as determined by DAC

variables.name: SOURCE_SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: Subject ID used in the Source Repository

tables.description: This sample attributes table contains sample IDs, body site, analyte type, and name of the centers which conducted genotyping or sequencing.

variables.name: BODY_SITE
variables.description: Body site where sample was collected

variables.name: GENOTYPING_CENTER
variables.description: Name of the center which conducted genotyping

variables.name: SEQUENCING_CENTER
variables.description: Name of the center which conducted sequencing

tables.description: This subject phenotype table contains subject IDs, sex, age, and SAB clonal complex which caused bacteremia.

variables.name: CC
variables.description: Bacteremia was caused by a single S. aureus bacterial clonal complex

tables.description: This data table contains a mapping of study subject IDs to sample IDs. Samples are the final preps submitted for genotyping, sequencing, and/or expression data. For example, if one patient (subject ID) gave one sample, and that sample was processed differently to generate 2 sequencing runs, there would be two rows, both using the same subject ID, but having 2 unique sample IDs. The data table also includes sample use.
